args.code:
import json
import re

# Get the business data from the previous query
business_data = var_functions.query_db:8

# Extract state information from descriptions
state_reviews = {}
business_states = {}

for business in business_data:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    business_id = business.get('business_id')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    
    # Extract state using regex pattern looking for city, STATE pattern
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        business_states[business_id] = state
        
        if state not in state_reviews:
            state_reviews[state] = 0
        state_reviews[state] += review_count

# Find state with highest review count
max_state = max(state_reviews, key=state_reviews.get)
max_reviews = state_reviews[max_state]

result = {
    'state_reviews': state_reviews,
    'max_state': max_state,
    'max_reviews': max_reviews,
    'business_states_sample': dict(list(business_states.items())[:5])
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import re

# Get the business data using locals() to access the stored result
business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:8']

# Extract state information from descriptions
state_reviews = {}
business_states = {}

for business in business_data:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    business_id = business.get('business_id')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    
    # Extract state using regex pattern looking for city, STATE pattern
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        business_states[business_id] = state
        
        if state not in state_reviews:
            state_reviews[state] = 0
        state_reviews[state] += review_count

# Find state with highest review count
if state_reviews:
    max_state = max(state_reviews, key=state_reviews.get)
    max_reviews = state_reviews[max_state]
else:
    max_state = None
    max_reviews = 0

result = {
    'state_reviews': state_reviews,
    'max_state': max_state,
    'max_reviews': max_reviews,
    'business_states_sample': dict(list(business_states.items())[:5])
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Get the business data using locals() to access the stored result
business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:8']

# Extract state information from descriptions
state_reviews = {}
business_states = {}

for business in business_data:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    business_id = business.get('business_id')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    
    # Extract state using regex pattern looking for city, STATE pattern
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        business_states[business_id] = state
        
        if state not in state_reviews:
            state_reviews[state] = 0
        state_reviews[state] += review_count

# Find state with highest review count
if state_reviews:
    max_state = max(state_reviews, key=state_reviews.get)
    max_reviews = state_reviews[max_state]
else:
    max_state = None
    max_reviews = 0

result = {
    'state_reviews': state_reviews,
    'max_state': max_state,
    'max_reviews': max_reviews,
    'business_states_sample': dict(list(business_states.items())[:5])
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

Test drove and purchased a used car on 3/31 for my 16 year old daughter, the salesman knew it was for her. Bought the 3 year 'bumper to bumper' warranty the finance guy said was the same as if it \"just came off the showroom floor\". They needed to remove pin stripes and fix a tire pressure sensor so they agreed to deliver it; we live almost two hours away. Met them after dark near the highway on 4/2; the check engine light came on on our way home. Texted a pic to the salesman and called him the next day -- no response. Left several voice mails for the warranty/finance guy - no response. Took it to a friend at local dealer who hooked it up to the diagnostic machine and said it was the after-market high-flow air intake that caused the warning light. I would not have purchased the car had I known that. Then another warning light - reduced engine performance, reduce clutch use. To make a very long story short - clutch goes, daughter gets stranded on busy highway, vehicle towed back to Ford of Port Richey; lots of promises about fixing it and being at the front of the service line, zero follow up, 53 days of ownership, 31 days out of commission/in shop and the story ends with a bill for $2000, even though they ultimately acknowledged one of their managers drove the car for three days because the check engine light kept coming on before they sold it. I won't even mention the cracked windshield we saw the morning after they delivered - just turn it in on your insurance. I understand it is free but still counts as a claim.   I'm just glad 16 year old girls are patient and understanding.", "date": "08 Jun 2019, 07:10"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1928", "user_id": "userid_1151", "business_ref": "businessref_9", "rating": "5", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "I had a great experience at Coffee House Too. I came on a Sunday around brunch and there was no wait for a table, and the service was exceptional. As you wait for your food, you are offered a complimentary mimosa as well as a muffin. On top of that, the cost for food is relatively low compared to other brunch locations. var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import re

# Get business data - this returns only 5 rows based on the queries
business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:40']

# Extract state information and sum review counts by state
state_reviews = {}
business_to_state = {}

for business in business_data:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    business_id = business.get('business_id')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    
    # Extract state from description
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        business_to_state[business_id] = state
        
        if state not in state_reviews:
            state_reviews[state] = 0
        state_reviews[state] += review_count

# Find state with highest total reviews
if state_reviews:
    top_state = max(state_reviews, key=state_reviews.get)
    top_state_reviews = state_reviews[top_state]
else:
    top_state = None
    top_state_reviews = 0

result = {
    'state_reviews': state_reviews,
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_state_reviews': top_state_reviews,
    'business_to_state': business_to_state
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Get business data - this returns only 5 rows based on the queries
business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:40']

# Extract state information and sum review counts by state
state_reviews = {}
business_to_state = {}

for business in business_data:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    business_id = business.get('business_id')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    
    # Extract state from description
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        business_to_state[business_id] = state
        
        if state not in state_reviews:
            state_reviews[state] = 0
        state_reviews[state] += review_count

# Find state with highest total reviews
if state_reviews:
    top_state = max(state_reviews, key=state_reviews.get)
    top_state_reviews = state_reviews[top_state]
else:
    top_state = None
    top_state_reviews = 0

result = {
    'state_reviews': state_reviews,
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_state_reviews': top_state_reviews,
    'business_to_state': business_to_state
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import re

# Get the review data from the file
review_file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']
with open(review_file_path, 'r') as f:
    reviews_data = json.load(f)

# Convert business_id to the format used in review table
business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:46']
state_reviews = {}
state_ratings = {}
business_state_map = {}

# Map business_id to state and track review counts/ratings by state
for business in business_data:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    business_id = business.get('business_id')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    
    # Extract state from description
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        business_state_map[business_id] = state
        
        # Initialize state tracking
        if state not in state_reviews:
            state_reviews[state] = review_count
            state_ratings[state] = []
        else:
            state_reviews[state] += review_count

# Map review ratings to states
for review in reviews_data:
    business_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    if not business_ref:
        continue
    
    # Convert business_ref (businessref_X) to business_id (businessid_X)
    business_id_num = business_ref.replace('businessref_', '')
    business_id = f'businessid_{business_id_num}'
    
    rating = int(review.get('rating', 0))
    
    # Find state for this business
    if business_id in business_state_map:
        state = business_state_map[business_id]
        state_ratings[state].append(rating)

# Calculate average ratings by state
state_avg_ratings = {}
for state, ratings in state_ratings.items():
    if ratings:
        state_avg_ratings[state] = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)
    else:
        state_avg_ratings[state] = 0

# Find state with highest review count
if state_reviews:
    top_state = max(state_reviews, key=state_reviews.get)
    top_state_review_count = state_reviews[top_state]
    top_state_avg_rating = state_avg_ratings.get(top_state, 0)
else:
    top_state = None
    top_state_review_count = 0
    top_state_avg_rating = 0

result = {
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_state_review_count': top_state_review_count,
    'top_state_avg_rating': round(top_state_avg_rating, 2),
    'all_states_reviews': state_reviews,
    'all_states_avg_ratings': {k: round(v, 2) for k, v in state_avg_ratings.items()}
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Get the review data from the file
review_file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']
with open(review_file_path, 'r') as f:
    reviews_data = json.load(f)

# Convert business_id to the format used in review table
business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:46']
state_reviews = {}
state_ratings = {}
business_state_map = {}

# Map business_id to state and track review counts/ratings by state
for business in business_data:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    business_id = business.get('business_id')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    
    # Extract state from description
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        business_state_map[business_id] = state
        
        # Initialize state tracking
        if state not in state_reviews:
            state_reviews[state] = review_count
            state_ratings[state] = []
        else:
            state_reviews[state] += review_count

# Map review ratings to states
for review in reviews_data:
    business_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    if not business_ref:
        continue
    
    # Convert business_ref (businessref_X) to business_id (businessid_X)
    business_id_num = business_ref.replace('businessref_', '')
    business_id = f'businessid_{business_id_num}'
    
    rating = int(review.get('rating', 0))
    
    # Find state for this business
    if business_id in business_state_map:
        state = business_state_map[business_id]
        state_ratings[state].append(rating)

# Calculate average ratings by state
state_avg_ratings = {}
for state, ratings in state_ratings.items():
    if ratings:
        state_avg_ratings[state] = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)
    else:
        state_avg_ratings[state] = 0

# Find state with highest review count
if state_reviews:
    top_state = max(state_reviews, key=state_reviews.get)
    top_state_review_count = state_reviews[top_state]
    top_state_avg_rating = state_avg_ratings.get(top_state, 0)
else:
    top_state = None
    top_state_review_count = 0
    top_state_avg_rating = 0

result = {
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_state_review_count': top_state_review_count,
    'top_state_avg_rating': round(top_state_avg_rating, 2),
    'all_states_reviews': state_reviews,
    'all_states_avg_ratings': {k: round(v, 2) for k, v in state_avg_ratings.items()}
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

# Get the review data
review_file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']
with open(review_file_path, 'r') as f:
    reviews_data = json.load(f)

# Initialize state tracking
state_reviews = {}
state_ratings = {}
business_state_map = {}

# Map business_id to state and collect review counts
for business in business_data:
    business_id = business.get('business_id')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    description = business.get('description', '')
    
    # Extract state
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        business_state_map[business_id] = state
        
        # Aggregate review counts by state
        if state not in state_reviews:
            state_reviews[state] = 0
        state_reviews[state] += review_count
        
        # Initialize ratings list for state
        if state not in state_ratings:
            state_ratings[state] = []

# Extract ratings and map to states
for review in reviews_data:
    business_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    if not business_ref:
        continue
    
    # Convert businessref_X to businessid_X
    business_id_num = business_ref.replace('businessref_', '')
    business_id = f'businessid_{business_id_num}'
    
    # Only process if this business is in our state map
    if business_id in business_state_map:
        state = business_state_map[business_id]
        rating = int(review.get('rating', 0))
        state_ratings[state].append(rating)

# Find state with highest review count
top_state = max(state_reviews, key=state_reviews.get) if state_reviews else None
top_state_review_count = state_reviews.get(top_state, 0)

# Calculate average rating for top state
top_state_avg_rating = 0
if top_state and state_ratings[top_state]:
    top_state_avg_rating = sum(state_ratings[top_state]) / len(state_ratings[top_state])

# Also calculate average ratings for all states
all_states_avg_ratings = {}
for state, ratings in state_ratings.items():
    if ratings:
        all_states_avg_ratings[state] = round(sum(ratings) / len(ratings), 2)
    else:
        all_states_avg_ratings[state] = 0.0

# Calculate average rating separately for top state
top_state_avg_rating_rounded = round(top_state_avg_rating, 2) if top_state else 0.0

result = {
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_state_review_count': top_state_review_count,
    'top_state_avg_rating': top_state_avg_rating_rounded,
    'all_states_reviews': state_reviews,
    'all_states_avg_ratings': all_states_avg_ratings
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Get the business data
business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:56']

# Get the review data
review_file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']
with open(review_file_path, 'r') as f:
    reviews_data = json.load(f)

# Initialize state tracking
state_reviews = {}
state_ratings = {}
business_state_map = {}

# Map business_id to state and collect review counts
for business in business_data:
    business_id = business.get('business_id')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    description = business.get('description', '')
    
    # Extract state
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        business_state_map[business_id] = state
        
        # Aggregate review counts by state
        if state not in state_reviews:
            state_reviews[state] = 0
        state_reviews[state] += review_count
        
        # Initialize ratings list for state
        if state not in state_ratings:
            state_ratings[state] = []

# Extract ratings and map to states
for review in reviews_data:
    business_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    if not business_ref:
        continue
    
    # Convert businessref_X to businessid_X
    business_id_num = business_ref.replace('businessref_', '')
    business_id = f'businessid_{business_id_num}'
    
    # Only process if this business is in our state map
    if business_id in business_state_map:
        state = business_state_map[business_id]
        rating = int(review.get('rating', 0))
        state_ratings[state].append(rating)

# Find state with highest review count
top_state = max(state_reviews, key=state_reviews.get) if state_reviews else None
top_state_review_count = state_reviews.get(top_state, 0)

# Calculate average rating for top state
top_state_avg_rating = 0
if top_state and state_ratings[top_state]:
    top_state_avg_rating = sum(state_ratings[top_state]) / len(state_ratings[top_state])

# Also calculate average ratings for all states
all_states_avg_ratings = {}
for state, ratings in state_ratings.items():
    if ratings:
        all_states_avg_ratings[state] = round(sum(ratings) / len(ratings), 2)
    else:
        all_states_avg_ratings[state] = 0.0

# Calculate average rating separately for top state
top_state_avg_rating_rounded = round(top_state_avg_rating, 2) if top_state else 0.0

result = {
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_state_review_count': top_state_review_count,
    'top_state_avg_rating': top_state_avg_rating_rounded,
    'all_states_reviews': state_reviews,
    'all_states_avg_ratings': all_states_avg_ratings
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

import json

    'state_review_counts': {'CA': 8, 'MO': 81, 'ID': 39, 'FL': 5, 'IN': 28}
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened
